src_embedding_text: source: design test case id: tc-authentication-001 title: successful user login with existing token module: authentication source files or pages: 1, 3 covered methods: preconditions: - user has a valid token stored locally. - firebase authentication service is operational. test data: - valid token: `"abc123xyz456"` - user email: `"user@example.com"` test steps: 1. main process calls `gettoken()` on localuser. 2. localuser returns the stored token (`"abc123xyz456"`). 3. localuser sends the token to authentication service via `request(token)`. 4. authentication verifies the token and returns `status = "success"`. 5. authentication requests user data (`dati utente`). 6. localuser creates a new `localuser` instance with fetched data. 7. authentication sends `"done"` confirmation. expected result: - localuser instance is created successfully. - user is logged in with access to all authenticated features. - no error messages or crashes. unit: project:

src_embedding_text: source: design test case id: tc-authentication-002 title: failed login with invalid token module: authentication source files or pages: 1, 3 covered methods: preconditions: - user has an invalid/expired token stored locally. - firebase authentication service is operational. test data: - invalid token: `"invalidtoken123"` - user email: `"user@example.com"` test steps: 1. main process calls `gettoken()` on localuser. 2. localuser returns the invalid token (`"invalidtoken123"`). 3. localuser sends the token to authentication service via `request(token)`. 4. authentication rejects the token and returns `status = "failure"`. 5. localuser does **not** create a new `localuser` instance. expected result: - authentication process fails gracefully. - user remains unauthenticated; no crash or data leakage. - system prompts user to re-authenticate (e.g., login screen). unit: project:
